"Optimizing tool selection... I'll read the backend controller and DTO files to extract routes and payload shapes for the frontend prompt. Progress update: I extracted controllers and DTOs; next I'll mark the TODOs completed and produce the frontend AI prompt. Frontend AI Prompt Build a simple Create React App (CRA) frontend for the provided Java Spring Boot backend. Constraints: use only CRA (no Next/Remix/etc.), functional components + hooks, React Router for routing, no global state libraries (no Redux), minimal CSS, and use browser fetch for API calls. Use REACT_APP_API_BASE_URL env var (default http://localhost:8080). Keep the UI minimal and usable (lists, forms, basic validation). Backend summary (available endpoints & payloads — use these exactly): Auth POST /auth/login Request: { "username": "string", "password": "string" } Response: { "message": "string", "email": "string?", "role": "string?" } POST /auth/register Request: { "username": "string", "email": "string", "password": "string" } Response: 201 { "message": "string", "email": "string", "role": "string" } User POST /user/create — Request: User (send { username,email,password }) — Response: User GET /user/read/{id} — Response: User POST /user/update — Request: User DELETE /user/delete/{id} GET /user/all — Response: User[] Courses POST /courses/create — Request: { code, name, description, imageUrl } — Response: Courses GET /courses/read/{id} — Response: Courses PUT /courses/update — Request: Courses DELETE /courses/delete/{id} GET /courses/all — Response: Courses[] (each: id, code, name, description, imageUrl) Tutors POST /tutors/create — Request: { name, specialization, description, location, availableTime, imageUrl, course } (course may be object or id) — Response: Tutors GET /tutors/read/{id} — Response: Tutors PUT /tutors/update — Request: Tutors DELETE /tutors/delete/{id} GET /tutors/all — Response: Tutors[] (each: id, name, specialization, description, location, availableTime, imageUrl, course) Bookings POST /bookings/create — Request: { firstName,lastName,email,yearOfStudy,campus,level,bookingDate,tutorName } — Response: Bookings GET /bookings/read/{id} — Response: Bookings POST /bookings/update — Request: Bookings DELETE /bookings/delete/{id} GET /bookings/all — Response: Bookings[] GET /bookings/user/{email} — Response: Bookings[] for given email Review POST /api/review/create — Request: { name, feedback } — Response: Review GET /api/review/read/{id} — Response: Review GET /api/review/read-by-name/{name} — Response: Review? PUT /api/review/update — Request: Review DELETE /api/review/delete/{id} GET /api/review/all — Response: Review[] Suggested frontend pages/components (simple): Landing / Home: show featured courses or link to courses/tutors. Login and Register pages: calls /auth/login and /auth/register. Store logged-in email in localStorage (no JWT required). CoursesList: GET /courses/all, link to CourseDetail. CourseDetail: GET /courses/read/{id} + show tutors related (use /tutors/all and filter by course.id if available). TutorsList: GET /tutors/all. TutorDetail: GET /tutors/read/{id}, include a BookingForm that POSTs to /bookings/create. MyBookings: GET /bookings/user/{email} using logged-in email; allow delete. Reviews: GET /api/review/all, create review form POST /api/review/create. Implementation notes for the AI: Use fetch(${TutorFinderSystemBackend.) with JSON headers. Handle errors and show user-friendly messages. Provide sample request/response JSON examples in code comments for each API call. Keep components small and focused. Use React Router: routes /, /login, /register, /courses, /courses/:id, /tutors, /tutors/:id, /bookings, /reviews. No external state management: use component state and localStorage for current user email. Styling: basic responsive layout with CSS (no frameworks). Output: full file tree and file contents for the CRA app (e.g., src/index.js, src/App.js, src/pages/, src/components/, src/api.js, public/ files). The AI should return complete, copy-paste-ready files. Include a .env.example with: REACT_APP_API_BASE_URL=http://localhost:8080 Include run instructions to paste into terminal: npx create-react-app tutor-finder-frontend Replace/create files with provided files npm start Example minimal API call (for you to include in generated code): Login: fetch: body: JSON.stringify({ username, password }) on success store email from response: localStorage.setItem('userEmail', res.email) Edge cases / assumptions to clarify in code comments: Backend CORS allows http://localhost:3000. If course relation requires nested object on create, allow sending { id: <courseId> }. Dates: send bookingDate as ISO string. Deliverable format: Return a zip-like file tree listing and the full contents of each file (or the exact files to create), plus short run instructions"
